title: koyamacalia maximowicziana
intro: Koyamacalia maximowicziana, also known as the Maximowicz's Koyama chrysanthemum, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial plant native to Japan, Korea, and eastern China, traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and fever. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits such as relieving colds, coughs, and bronchitis. Herbal preparations of Koyamacalia maximowicziana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
